venues.name: de Young Museum
venues.address: 50 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco, CA 94118
venues.notes: Closed Mondays. Friday evening extended hours currently on summer hiatus (resumes Sep 28). Amy has FAMSF membership — all permanent collection galleries and most ticketed exhibitions included.

current_exhibitions.title: Monet and Venice
current_exhibitions.description: The first exhibition dedicated exclusively to Monet's Venetian cityscapes, reuniting masterworks from his 1908 visit for the first time in over a century. Closes July 26 — time-sensitive.

current_exhibitions.title: Rose B. Simpson: LEXICON
current_exhibitions.description: First de Young solo for a contemporary Native American artist. Two customized classic cars painted with Pueblo pottery motifs plus a site-specific mural evoking the Southwest. Transforms Wilsey Court.

current_exhibitions.title: Boom and Bust: Photographing Northern California
current_exhibitions.description: Photographs chronicling cycles of urban settlement in Northern California, including post-1906 earthquake SF, bridge construction, and SoMa development.

current_exhibitions.title: Nengi Omuku: The Gathering
current_exhibitions.description: First U.S. museum show by Nigerian artist Nengi Omuku — eight paintings on handwoven sanyan cloth reimagining Lagos, in dialogue with sculptures and textiles from the FAMSF collection.

events.title: Free Saturdays at the de Young
events.description: Free general admission to permanent collection galleries for Bay Area residents (9-county area). Show Bay Area ID. Special exhibitions still require a ticket (included with membership).
events.cost_notes: free for Bay Area residents; included with FAMSF membership

events.title: Docents in the Galleries: Monet and Venice
events.description: Docents available in the Monet and Venice galleries Tue–Sun at 11:30 AM and 1:30 PM. No reservation required. Last chance — closes July 26.

events.title: Highlights Tour at the de Young
events.description: Docent-led highlights tour Tue–Sun. Meet at the electronic sign in the main lobby. No reservation required.

venues.name: Legion of Honor
venues.notes: Closed Mondays. Amy has FAMSF membership — all permanent collection and special exhibitions included.

current_exhibitions.title: The Etruscans: From the Heart of Ancient Italy
current_exhibitions.description: Nearly 200 objects from ancient Etruscan civilization — bronzes, terracotta, gold jewelry, and treasures never before seen in the U.S. On loan from Italian museums.

current_exhibitions.title: Drawn to Venice
current_exhibitions.description: 30+ drawings and prints celebrating the arts of Venice and the Veneto from Renaissance to Rococo. In dialogue with 'Monet and Venice' at the de Young.

current_exhibitions.title: Wayne Thiebaud: Art Comes from Art
current_exhibitions.description: About 65 Thiebaud works — cakes, people, cityscapes, mountain landscapes — alongside his reinterpretations of famous artworks from his personal collection.

current_exhibitions.title: Ferlinghetti for San Francisco
current_exhibitions.description: Explores Lawrence Ferlinghetti (1919–2021) as painter, draftsman, printmaker. Work combines image and text to explore isolation, violence, resilience. Logan Gallery. Closes July 19.

events.title: Free Saturdays at the Legion of Honor
events.description: Free general admission to permanent collection galleries for Bay Area residents (9-county area). Valid ID required.
events.cost_notes: free for Bay Area residents; included with FAMSF membership

events.title: Docent Tours: The Etruscans
events.description: Docent-led tours of The Etruscans exhibition Tue–Sun at 12:30 PM. Meet at the exhibition entrance on the lower level. About one hour.

events.title: Highlights Tour at the Legion of Honor
events.description: Docent-led highlights tour Tue–Sun. Meet in the rotunda. No reservation required.
